attractions.name: Cathedral of Santa Maria del Fiore (Duomo)
attractions.description: The iconic red dome dominates Florence's skyline. The intricate marble façade and sheer scale of the structure set the tone for the city's artistic grandeur
attractions.hours: Cathedral: 10:15 AM – 3:45 PM | Dome climb: 8:15 AM – 7:30 PM (Sunday Closed)

phrases: Buongiorno — Good morning/Good day (used until afternoon)
Buonasera — Good evening (used from late afternoon onwards)
Dress modestly when visiting churches - cover shoulders and knees, remove hats
Greet shopkeepers with 'Buongiorno' when entering and 'Arrivederci' when leaving
Avoid eating or drinking while walking near churches and monuments - it's considered disrespectful
Dov'è il bagno? — Where is the bathroom?
Il conto, per favore — The check, please
Dining happens later in Italy - lunch around 1-2 PM, dinner after 7:30 PM
safety_tips: INDIAN EMBASSY CONTACT: Consulate General of India in Milan (covers Florence) - Address: Via San Martino 7, 20122 Milan. Phone: +39-02-8053-4280. Email: cg.milan@mea.gov.in. Emergency helpline: +39-348-130-5611
EMERGENCY NUMBERS: Police (Carabinieri): 112 or 113. Ambulance/Medical Emergency: 118. Fire Department: 115. European Emergency Number: 112 (works from any phone)
TOURIST POLICE: Questura di Firenze - Via Zara 2, 50129 Florence. Phone: +39-055-49771. They have English-speaking officers and handle tourist-related issues

COMMON SCAMS TO AVOID: Fake petition signers near tourist sites who pickpocket while you're distracted. Overpriced restaurant menus without prices shown. Fake gold ring 'findings' where scammers claim you found jewelry. Friendship bracelet sellers who tie bracelets on your wrist then demand payment

PICKPOCKET HOTSPOTS: Be extra vigilant at Ponte Vecchio, Uffizi Gallery queues, Santa Maria Novella train station, crowded buses (especially lines 12 and 13), and around Duomo. Keep bags zipped and in front of you
MONEY SAFETY: Use ATMs inside banks when possible. Avoid displaying large amounts of cash. Be cautious of card skimming devices. Many places prefer cash, so carry small denominations
TRANSPORTATION SAFETY: Validate train/bus tickets to avoid fines. Keep copies of tickets until you exit stations. Beware of unofficial taxi drivers - use only licensed white taxis with meters
ACCOMMODATION SAFETY: Book through reputable platforms. Verify hotel addresses and contact details. Keep hotel business cards with you for easy navigation back

HEALTH PRECAUTIONS: Tap water is safe to drink. Carry basic medications as Indian medicines may not be available. European Health Insurance Card (EHIC) not applicable - ensure travel insurance covers medical expenses

DOCUMENT SECURITY: Keep passport copies separate from originals. Store digital copies in cloud storage. Register with Indian embassy upon arrival for longer stays. Carry ID at all times as required by Italian law
FOOD SAFETY: Eat at busy restaurants with high turnover. Be cautious of street food if you have a sensitive stomach. Restaurant service charges (coperto) are normal and legal

CULTURAL SENSITIVITY: Dress modestly when visiting churches (cover shoulders and knees). Many churches close during lunch hours (12:30-3:30 PM). Tipping is not mandatory but 10% is appreciated for good service
LANGUAGE HELP: Download offline translation apps. Learn basic Italian phrases: 'Aiuto' (Help), 'Chiama la polizia' (Call police), 'Ho bisogno di un medico' (I need a doctor)
TOURIST INFORMATION: Official tourist info at Via Cavour 1r. Phone: +39-055-290832. They provide maps, assistance, and can help contact authorities if needed
GENERAL SAFETY: Florence is generally safe, but stay alert in crowded areas. Avoid poorly lit areas at night. Trust your instincts - if something feels wrong, leave the area immediately

connectivity_tips: Purchase a Vodafone Italy SIM card at Florence Airport or Santa Maria Novella train station for €15-20 with 20-30GB data, offering excellent coverage throughout Tuscany
Buy TIM (Telecom Italia) prepaid SIM at tobacco shops (tabacchi) or TIM stores for €10-15 with good 4G coverage in Florence city center and tourist areas
Consider WindTre SIM cards available at supermarkets like Conad or Esselunga for €10-12, providing decent coverage and competitive data packages
Use eSIM providers like Airalo or Holafly before traveling - purchase 'Italy' or 'Europe' plans for €8-25 depending on data needs, activated instantly
Download the 'TIM Tourist' eSIM plan specifically designed for visitors, offering 30GB for €25 with 30-day validity and good network coverage
Bring your passport/ID when purchasing physical SIM cards as it's mandatory for registration in Italy
Activate roaming on your Indian SIM as backup - Airtel and Jio offer Italy roaming packs for ₹2999-4999 for 7-30 days
Free WiFi is available at major attractions like Uffizi Gallery, Palazzo Pitti, and most cafes, but coverage can be spotty in historic areas
Network coverage is excellent in Florence city center but may weaken in rural Tuscan areas during day trips to Chianti or Siena
Top up your Italian SIM at any tabacchi shop, supermarket, or online through provider apps - most accept cash or international cards
Consider Iliad SIM cards for longer stays (€7.99/month for 120GB) available at Iliad stores or online with good 4G+ coverage
Download offline maps on Google Maps or Maps.me before exploring, as data speeds may slow in crowded tourist areas during peak season

transport_options.description: Tram T1 connects Florence to surrounding areas including hotels
The centre of Florence is quite compact and walkable, most people get around on foot
transport_options._source_file: Italy/All Inclusive Guide- Aayush and Sonal- Switzerland and Italy.pdf

transport_options.description: Single ticket valid for 90 minutes, urban ticket for buses and trams
transport_options.cost: Single ticket €1.50, Urban ticket €1.70, Agile 10 card €14
transport_options._source_file: Italy/All Inclusive Guide- Aayush and Sonal- Switzerland and Italy.pdf

health_tips: No special vaccinations are required for Indian travelers visiting Florence, Italy, as it is not in a yellow fever endemic area and has no mandatory vaccine requirements for Indian passport holders.
Ensure routine vaccinations are up to date: MMR (measles, mumps, rubella), DPT (diphtheria, pertussis, tetanus), chickenpox, polio, and yearly flu shot.
Recommended vaccines include Hepatitis A and B, especially if you plan extended stays or have underlying health conditions.
Consider Typhoid vaccination if you plan to eat at smaller establishments or street food, though risk is generally low in Florence.
Tap water in Florence is safe to drink and meets EU standards. Bottled water is widely available if preferred.
Italian cuisine is generally safe, but practice normal food hygiene: eat hot, freshly cooked meals and avoid food that has been sitting out.
Gelato and ice cream from reputable establishments are safe to consume.
Pack a basic first aid kit with common medications, as some Indian medicines may not be readily available.
Bring adequate supplies of prescription medications in original packaging, along with a doctor's letter if needed.
European Health Insurance Card (EHIC) is not applicable for Indian citizens - ensure you have comprehensive travel health insurance.
Emergency medical care is excellent in Florence. Emergency number is 112 for ambulance services.
Summer temperatures can reach 35°C+ - stay hydrated, use sunscreen, and take breaks in shade during sightseeing.
Air quality in Florence city center can be poor due to traffic - those with respiratory conditions should carry inhalers.
Pharmacies (farmacia) are widely available and pharmacists can provide advice for minor ailments.
No special precautions needed for mosquito-borne diseases, but pack insect repellent for comfort during summer evenings.
